mnemonics: FLIP-FLOPS: Feedback Loops In Processors - For Latches, Outputs Persist Steadily
keyPoints: Sequential circuits have memory, unlike combinational circuits whose outputs depend only on current inputs.
The fundamental building blocks are latches and flip-flops, which store a single bit of state.
The circuit's output depends on both the current inputs and the previous state (history).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to initialize the state (e.g., using a reset signal) which can lead to an unknown starting point.
